verses.text: Pashur the priest strikes Jeremiah and puts him in the stocks; the prophet names him Magor-missabib, 'fear on every side.' Persecution meets the messenger. Those who silence God's word bring terror on themselves.

Pashur the priest strikes Jeremiah and puts him in the stocks; the prophet names him Magor-missabib, 'fear on every side.' Persecution meets the messenger. Those who silence God's word bring terror on themselves.

Pashur the priest strikes Jeremiah and puts him in the stocks; the prophet names him Magor-missabib, 'fear on every side.' Persecution meets the messenger. Those who silence God's word bring terror on themselves.
God declares Pashur shall become a terror to himself and his friends and go captive to Babylon. His false prophecies recoil. The persecutor who promised peace will himself be carried away.
God declares Pashur shall become a terror to himself and his friends and go captive to Babylon. His false prophecies recoil. The persecutor who promised peace will himself be carried away.
God declares Pashur shall become a terror to himself and his friends and go captive to Babylon. His false prophecies recoil. The persecutor who promised peace will himself be carried away.

Jeremiah complains he is mocked all day, yet 'his word was in mine heart as a burning fire shut up in my bones'; he cannot keep silent. The word compels him. God's truth burns within the faithful, stronger than every discouragement.

Jeremiah complains he is mocked all day, yet 'his word was in mine heart as a burning fire shut up in my bones'; he cannot keep silent. The word compels him. God's truth burns within the faithful, stronger than every discouragement.

Jeremiah complains he is mocked all day, yet 'his word was in mine heart as a burning fire shut up in my bones'; he cannot keep silent. The word compels him. God's truth burns within the faithful, stronger than every discouragement.

Surrounded by whisperers waiting for his fall, he is sure 'the LORD is with me as a mighty terrible one,' so his foes shall stumble. God is his champion. The persecuted prophet rests in the strength of his God.

Surrounded by whisperers waiting for his fall, he is sure 'the LORD is with me as a mighty terrible one,' so his foes shall stumble. God is his champion. The persecuted prophet rests in the strength of his God.

He calls on the LORD 'that triest the righteous,' then bursts into praise: 'Sing unto the LORD... for he hath delivered the soul of the poor.' Trust turns to song. Faith can praise even in the midst of trouble.

He calls on the LORD 'that triest the righteous,' then bursts into praise: 'Sing unto the LORD... for he hath delivered the soul of the poor.' Trust turns to song. Faith can praise even in the midst of trouble.
Yet he sinks again, cursing the day of his birth and asking why he was born to see only sorrow. His grief is raw. Even a great prophet may pour out the darkest despair, and God bears with it.
